race.name: GFNY Punta del Este
race.tagline: 146km of beach glamour meets brutal hills in Uruguay's sexiest sufferfest.

vitals.location: Punta del Este, Maldonado Department, Uruguay
vitals.field_size: Intimate GFNY field of hundreds, drawing South American elites and sun-seeking internationals
vitals.registration: Entry locks in elite chip timing, mandatory NYC-designed Italian jersey, and finisher's medal toward multi-event glory
vitals.prize_purse: GFNY age-group podiums plus South American Championship perks like free NYC flights for overall winners
vitals.aid_stations: Fully stocked GFNY feeds with post-race beach club feast and awards
vitals.cutoff_time: Sag wagon sweeps ensure all finishers medal, no DNF shame
distance_options: Long Competitive: 90.7mi, 5423ft — Hilly beast for elites chasing qualifiers

climate.description: March delivers Punta del Este's steamy Southern Hemisphere sendoff with temps pushing 85°F and relentless coastal winds. Late summer heat bakes the beaches while ocean breezes offer fleeting mercy. Hydration is non-negotiable amid the glamour.

terrain.primary: Flat-to-hilly coastal crusher with wind-whipped drama
terrain.surface: Silky paved paradise from beachfront boulevards to smooth inland climbs. Police escort tames traffic for pro-peloton flow. Luxury villa views mask the sneaky suffering.

gravel_god_rating.tier_note: Solid T2 for exotic beach-hill combo punching above GFNY weight class. Adventure and experience shine in paradise setting, but shallow fields and heat cap elite appeal. Prestige grows as South American classic solidifies.

logistics.explanation: Overnight flights from North America land you jet-lag free in beachfront bliss. Expo at swanky Gorlero and beach club start/finish scream luxury. GFNY's machine ensures seamless packet pickup and police-escorted roads—zero excuses for logistical fails.

length.explanation: 146km delivers full GFNY suffering without century excess, perfect for early-season sharpeners. Long enough to shatter souls on hills, short enough for family beach time post-race. Elite distance with recreational bailout keeps it accessible yet brutal.

technicality.explanation: Paved dream with zero gravel grit or sketchy descents—pure road rocket. Winds add chaos, but smooth surfaces let watts fly unchecked. Beginners cruise, pros attack; no tech nightmares to spike your heart rate.

elevation.explanation: 1654m isn't Alpe d'Huez, but El Chorro and Punta Ballena pack early punches that splinter groups. Moderate overall with sneaky rollers keeps legs guessing amid ocean views. Solid vert for a beach bash, not mountain masochism.

climate.explanation: Hot-as-hell March scorches demand ice-vests and endless bottles, but coastal winds cool the burn. Late-summer glory risks pop-up storms—pack arm warmers for irony. Paradise weather with punishing edge elevates the pro-for-a-day vibe.

altitude.explanation: Sea-level snooze at 30m max—no hypoxia heroes here. Pure power test without thin-air excuses. Keeps focus on course cruelty, not oxygen debt.

adventure.explanation: Beaches to ranches, pine forests to luxury pads—Uruguay's Pearl of the Atlantic is cycling porn. Wild coastal loops and winery ties scream bucket-list epic. This ain't Kansas; it's sun-soaked suffer with St. Tropez swagger.

prestige.explanation: GFNY staple for South American aces, with NYC qualifier slots sweetening the pot. Growing rep as beach-circuit boss, but global elites skip for bigger fish. Solid regional cred without Tour de France buzz.

race_quality.explanation: GFNY polish shines: chip timing, podium jets, mandatory kit that turns civilians pro. Redesigned courses yearly keep it fresh and fierce. Police moderation and beach awards? Chef's kiss execution.

experience.explanation: Post-race beach club feasts amid champagne vibes—pure hedonism. Family-friendly resort with happy hours and winery links elevates beyond mere miles. You'll Instagram this harder than your Strava KOM.

community.explanation: Tight-knit LatAm peloton bonds over shared summer suffering. GFNY global tribe adds flavor, but fields skew regional without massive international draw. Solid vibes, room to grow the party.

field_depth.explanation: Hundreds strong with age-group sharks and regional pros sprinting for glory. Lacks Euro depth or NYC chaos, but South American champs ensure competitive bite. Qualifier corral fodder aplenty.

value.explanation: $130 snags jersey, medal, and paradise pain—steal for the spectacle. Packages amp luxury without wallet wreckage. Beats Euro rip-offs with beach bliss baked in.

expenses.explanation: Cheap flights from Americas, but resort markup stings on hotels and steakhouses. GFNY entry bargain offsets the glamour tax. Budget for uruguayo asado or regret the FOMO.

biased_opinion.summary: GFNY Punta del Este fuses St. Tropez glamour with sneaky South American hills, turning beach bums into qualifiers. Winds howl off Playa Mansa while Punta Ballena snaps the elastic—pure pro drama in paradise. Redesigned yearly, it's the GFNY you didn't know you craved. Families lounge while you suffer; post-race feasts seal the legend. Skip if flats bore you—this undulating beast demands respect.
biased_opinion.bottom_line: Exotic GFNY gem for early-season glory. Pin it if you crave coastal crushers; dodge if mountains are your mistress.

final_verdict.one_liner: Uruguay's hilly beach party punches way above its vert.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Yes if you're chasing qualifiers in sun-soaked style or opening North American legs jet-lag free. Skip if deep fields or mega climbs define your fix—stick to Euros for that.
final_verdict.alternatives: GFNY Punta Cana for Caribbean flats, GFNY Cozumel for island rollers, GFNY Los Cabos for Mexico heat.

logistics.airport: Fly into Punta del Este's PDP for 20-min beach transfers or Montevideo's MVD for budget hauls. Directs from Miami/Buenos Aires keep it painless.
logistics.lodging_strategy: Crash beachfront at Parador Pedro Figari or AWA Boutique—walkable to start/expo. Book early for March peak; Airbnbs slash costs inland.
logistics.food: Uruguayan parrilladas fuel the fire—stock up on asado and malbec. Race feeds handle the ride; post-beach feasts await victors.
logistics.packet_pickup: Expo at Espacio Gorlero Friday-Saturday grabs your Italian jersey and chip.
logistics.parking: Plenty at beach club start; GFNY corrals bikes securely.

history.founder: GFNY Global Endurance Sports Series under Uli Fluhme's vision
history.origin_story: Launched in 2018 to conquer South America's glitziest coast, GFNY Punta del Este imported NYC's pro-for-a-day ethos to Uruguay's Pearl of the Atlantic. Beach starts met inland hills, drawing LatAm riders hungry for qualifiers. Evolving courses cemented it as a Southern summer classic, blending luxury with lung-burning reality.
history.reputation: GFNY's beach-circuit crown jewel for wind-battered warriors. Riders rave about the vibe; elites target for regional hardware.

course_description.character: Playa Mansa erupts into 22km coastal flats before El Chorro's 800m hammer splits the bunch. Pine forests and wild beaches yield to Punta Ballena's iconic sting, looping back via Piriápolis oceanway. Interbalnearia finale panoramas reward survivors amid luxury ranches and lakes—windy, wavy, wildly scenic road poetry.

suffering_zones.label: El Chorro Selection
suffering_zones.desc: Early 800m climb shreds the peloton before beaches beckon. Short but savage gradient for breakaway births. Police escort keeps it racing-line pure.

suffering_zones.label: Punta Ballena Icon
suffering_zones.desc: Signature ascent past pine groves and mansions—view for glory or agony. Mid-course morale crusher with panoramic payback. Elites crest flying; mortals grind grinning.

suffering_zones.label: Piriápolis Coastway
suffering_zones.desc: 7km ocean-hugging rollers test fatigue amid crashing waves. Headwinds howl; legs scream for mercy. Beauty battles brutality to the line.

course_description.signature_challenge: Punta Ballena's mid-race bite atop early El Chorro damage—where groups shatter and solo quests ignite. Winds amplify the isolation, turning paradise into personal hell.

guide_variables.race_terrain: Coastal flats to hilly inland with ocean winds
guide_variables.race_weather: Hot late-summer with coastal breezes and potential storms

non_negotiables.requirement: 12+ hours endurance base with 3x hill repeats weekly
non_negotiables.why: 146km demands sustained power for wind and vert without cracking. Hills mimic El Chorro/Ballena to build snap. Heat acclimation via long hot rides prevents March meltdown.

non_negotiables.requirement: Nail 4hr zone 2 coastal simulator with 20min threshold bursts
non_negotiables.why: Replicates Piriápolis wind-whipping finales. Threshold for late-race surges. Jersey mandatory—train in aero kit for real-world feel.
